available_for_work.sh

Hi, I'm Your Name

Cyber Security EngineerEthical HackerPenetration TesterSecurity ResearcherBug Bounty Hunter
$ specialization --focus=

Breaking systems ethically to help build stronger ones. Focused on offensive security, web application testing, and continuous hands-on research.

Download ResumeContact MeGitHubLinkedIn

01. // about

About Me

$ cat about_me.txt

I'm a cybersecurity engineer focused on offensive security — breaking things responsibly so they can be built back stronger. My work spans ethical hacking, web application security, and vulnerability assessment, with a strong foundation in secure coding, Linux internals, and networking. I spend my time hunting bugs, building tooling, and documenting what I learn, because in this field the moment you stop learning is the moment you fall behind.

Ethical Hacking

Authorized offensive testing to uncover exploitable weaknesses before attackers do.

Web App Security

Deep focus on OWASP Top 10, business logic flaws, and modern web attack surfaces.

Vulnerability Assessment

Structured methodology for identifying, validating, and prioritizing risk.

Penetration Testing

End-to-end engagements: recon, exploitation, privilege escalation, reporting.

Secure Coding

Building and reviewing code with a security-first mindset from the ground up.

Linux & Networking

Comfortable at the command line, from packet captures to kernel-level detail.

Continuous Learning

Always in a lab, a CTF, or a writeup — the field moves fast and so do I.

03. // projects

Featured Work

Tooling

Subdomain Takeover Scanner

2026

A CLI tool that automates passive enumeration and CNAME fingerprinting to flag potentially vulnerable subdomains across large scopes.

PythonDNSRequestsAsyncio
Source
Web Pentest

Web App Pentest Report — E-Commerce Client

2026

Full black-box assessment covering authentication flaws, IDOR, and stored XSS with a remediation-focused report.

05. // journey

Learning Journey

2021

First Steps

Discovered ethical hacking through CTFs and started learning Linux fundamentals.

2022

Foundations

Built networking and scripting fundamentals; completed first vulnerable-machine walkthroughs.

2023

Specialization

Focused on web application security and started participating in bug bounty programs.

2024

Certification & Community

Earned eJPT, began publishing writeups, and joined the local security community.

2025

Professional Growth

Delivered first client pentest engagements and expanded into Active Directory attacks.

2026

Today

Working toward OSCP while contributing to open-source security tooling.

06. // experience

Experience & Education

Junior Penetration Tester

2025 — Present

SecureNet Consulting Remote

  • Conducted black-box and gray-box web application assessments for clients across fintech and e-commerce.
  • Authored professional-grade findings reports with CVSS scoring and remediation guidance.
  • Collaborated with blue team counterparts on detection validation post-engagement.

Security Research Intern

2024 — 2025

CyberLabs Hybrid

  • Researched emerging CVEs and reproduced proof-of-concept exploits in isolated lab environments.
  • Built internal tooling to automate recon phases of engagements, cutting setup time significantly.

education

B.Sc. in Computer Science / Cyber Security

Your University

2022 — 2026 · Your City, Country

Focused coursework in network security, cryptography, and systems programming.

08. // blog

Latest Writeups

Web SecurityOWASP

Understanding IDOR: Beyond the Basics

A practical look at how Insecure Direct Object References slip past code review and how to hunt for them systematically.

Jun 2026 6 min read
ReconBug Bounty

My Subdomain Enumeration Methodology

The passive and active recon workflow I use before touching a single endpoint during bug bounty hunting.

May 2026 8 min read
Active DirectoryRed Team

Active Directory Attacks for Beginners

Breaking down Kerberoasting, LLMNR poisoning, and BloodHound analysis for people new to AD security.

Apr 2026 10 min read
